title: How we responded to this season's flooding in Raigad district
author: Priya Nair
excerpt: Within 48 hours of the flood alert, volunteer teams were on the ground distributing food and clean water to over 400 families.
body:
When the flood alert came in early on a Tuesday morning, our volunteer coordination group activated the emergency response plan we'd rehearsed twice this year.

By that evening, three teams were on the ground in Raigad district, working alongside local authorities to identify the worst-affected households. Over the following four days, we distributed dry rations, clean drinking water and basic hygiene kits to more than 400 families.

This kind of response is only possible because of the volunteer network we've built over the past three years, and because of donors who keep our emergency fund ready before disaster strikes, not after.

If you'd like to support the next response, our emergency relief fund is always open.

title: 310 girls and counting: a look at our scholarship program's results
author: Dr. Anjali Mehra
excerpt: Eight years on, our Girls' Scholarship Program has helped over 300 girls stay in school past the critical class 8 dropout point.
body:
In 2018, we started the Girls' Scholarship Program with eleven girls in two villages. The idea was simple: cover the costs that push families to pull daughters out of school, and pair each girl with a mentor who checks in regularly.

Eight years later, 310 girls have come through the program. Early results are encouraging: of the first cohort, 80% completed secondary school, well above the local average for girls in similar circumstances.

We're now working on a small alumnae network so graduates can mentor the next group coming through.

title: Our 12th learning center opens its doors
author: Imran Sheikh
excerpt: A new center in Karjat brings consistent after-school tutoring to another 120 children.
body:
This month we opened our twelfth community learning center, this time in Karjat, after nearly a year of working with the local panchayat to secure a space.

The center will run six days a week, staffed by two trained local tutors, and will serve roughly 120 children from nearby hamlets who previously had no after-school support at all.

Thank you to everyone who contributed to making this center possible, including the volunteers who spent two weekends getting the space ready before launch.
